originalTitle: South African police chief suspended over $20m health contract
title: South African police chief suspended over $20m health contract
content: Cyril Ramaphosa said the suspension was because of the critical role Fannie Masemola plays in fighting crime.

originalTitle: More than 500 people killed in Tanzania election violence, inquiry finds
title: Friends and Fairness in Tanzania!
content:
Hello, my bright stars! I'm your African news reporter, ready to share some important news with you today. We're going to talk about a wonderful country called Tanzania. It's a place with sunny beaches and amazing animals!

What are Elections?

Sometimes, grown-ups in a country need to pick new helpers. These helpers are called leaders. They help decide how our towns and countries will be run. It's a bit like when you and your friends choose who will be the leader for a game. Everyone gets a chance to say who they think will be the best leader. This important way of choosing leaders is called an 'election'. It happens in many countries, including our friends in Tanzania. When there's an election, every grown-up gets to vote. Voting means they write down or mark who they want to be their leader. It's a very big job! Did you know? In some places, elections happen every few years! This gives people new chances to choose their leaders.

Why Fairness is Super Important

When we play games, we always want things to be fair, right? No one likes it when someone cheats or isn't kind. Elections need to be super fair too. Every grown-up should feel safe when they go to vote. They should not feel scared. Fairness means that everyone has an equal chance. It means that the choices people make are respected. When elections are fair, everyone feels happy and trusts the new leaders. This helps the country stay peaceful and strong. Imagine if your teacher chose only their favorite student to be class monitor every time – that wouldn't be fair, would it? So, elections must be fair for everyone.

Sad News from Tanzania

A little while ago, Tanzania had one of these important elections. Most times, elections are very peaceful and happy events. But sometimes, things can get a bit difficult. During the election time in Tanzania, some very sad things happened. There was some fighting and unhappiness. Sadly, some people got hurt during this time. This is called 'violence'. Violence is when people hurt each other, and it makes everyone very sad and scared. We never want violence. A special group was asked to look into what happened. They wanted to understand why people got hurt and how to stop it from happening again.

What the Special Group Found Out

This special group worked very hard, like super detectives! They looked at many clues and talked to many people. They wrote a big report about what they found. The report said that many, many people were hurt. It was a very big number, more than 500 people. This is a very sad thing to hear. The report also talked about who might have caused the problems. Some people in Tanzania, from groups called 'opposition parties' who wanted different leaders, thought that the police or the army might have been involved in the fighting. These are called 'security forces'. But the report, after looking at everything, didn't say exactly who was to blame. It was still a bit unclear who started the problems. Did you know? When an official group looks into something, it's called an 'inquiry'. They try to find out the truth!

Working Towards Peace

When sad things happen, it's important to learn from them. We all want to make sure that everyone is safe in Tanzania, always. Peace means no more fighting. It means everyone can smile and play and go to school without worry. Leaders in Tanzania and many kind people are working very hard to make sure elections are always peaceful and fair in the future. They want to build a country where everyone feels safe, loved, and respected. Peace is like sunshine for a country – it helps everything grow beautifully!

Why is Peace So Important for Kids?

When there is peace, kids like you can go to school every day. You can learn and play with your friends. Families can stay together and feel safe in their homes. Everyone can go to markets and visit their neighbors. Peace helps a country get stronger and better. It helps build hospitals and schools. It helps grow food for everyone. Did you know? Tanzania has amazing wildlife parks, like the Serengeti, where many animals like lions and zebras live! We want people to live peacefully too.

How We Can All Help

Even though you are small, you can still help make the world a peaceful place! Be kind to your friends at school. Share your toys. Listen to your teachers and parents. Always try to be fair when you play games. When you see someone sad, give them a hug or a kind word. These small actions make a big difference in our world! Every act of kindness helps to build a more peaceful world for everyone. It makes our communities stronger. Remember that Tanzania is a beautiful country with kind people who want peace. Just like in your own home, being kind and fair helps everyone be happy.

Our Hopes for Tanzania

We hope that Tanzania will always have fair elections where everyone feels safe and can make their choices freely. We send our biggest wishes for peace and happiness to all the children and families in Tanzania. Let's keep hoping for a future where everyone can live, learn, and play in a peaceful land, full of smiles and friendship. Thank you for listening, my wonderful friends! Until next time, stay safe and be kind!
summary: A special group in Tanzania looked into problems during an election and found that many people were hurt, but they couldn't say exactly who was responsible; now everyone wants peace and fairness for the future.
whyMatters: It's important for everyone in Tanzania to live safely and to have fair elections so they can choose their leaders without fear, helping the country be strong and happy for all its people, especially children.

originalTitle: South African court orders Zambia to return former president's body
title: South African court orders Zambia to return former president's body
content: Zambia authorities and Edgar Lungu's family are in a dispute over where the former leader's remains should be buried.

originalTitle: No Fifa plans for Iran-Italy swap at World Cup despite Trump envoy's suggestion
title: No Fifa plans for Iran-Italy swap at World Cup despite Trump envoy's suggestion
content: Fifa stands by comments made last week by president Gianni Infantino in which he said that "the Iranian team is coming, for sure".
